summary: The FOMC meeting, which kicked off today and concludes tomorrow, has Wall Street focused on how the Fed characterizes spiking energy prices and their impact on inflation amid the ongoing Middle East conflict. With a hold near-certain at 92%+, the real market mover is the updated dot plot and SEP, which could show downward GDP revisions and upward inflation projections — deepening the stagflation bind already pressuring equities and bonds.
tweet: FOMC meeting today isn't about a rate move — it's about whether the Fed can hold inflation expectations while geopolitical risk rises.
